meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve 'Can't Finish' Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Learn how to implement data-driven analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your project and validate your revenue.
content.answer_block: You aren't failing to finish because of code; you're failing because of a lack of feedback loops. By implementing automated analytics, you transform your MVP from a guessing game into a data-backed machine. This setup forces measurable progress, kills feature creep, and provides the psychological win needed to ship.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with projects that were 'almost done.' You stare at the codebase, feeling the crushing weight of feature creep, unsure if your next commit actually moves the needle. The silence of zero user feedback is the fastest way to kill your motivation and your startup.

But what if you could trade that uncertainty for cold, hard data? By integrating a robust analytics layer directly into your development workflow, you stop building in the dark. This isn't just about tracking clicks—it's about building a telemetry-first architecture that validates your product decisions in real-time, ensuring you ship only what matters.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single action that confirms a user finds value. This prevents scope creep by aligning every feature to this metric.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS MVP scope and suggest a single 'North Star Metric' based on a B2B productivity tool. Define the events I need to track to validate this metric.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a tracking_plan.md file documenting the North Star Metric and key user events identified.'

steps.title: Implement Event Schema Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized event dispatcher to decouple your core business logic from your tracking vendor, preventing vendor lock-in.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, TypeScript-based event dispatcher class that accepts event names and payloads, then routes them to a mock analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/lib/analytics.ts --content 'Implement the event dispatcher class from Antigravity.'

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Flows
steps.description: Place tracking hooks at the exact points where users encounter your value proposition. Keep it lean; focus on conversion bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the top 3 high-friction points in a standard SaaS onboarding flow and generate the code snippets to instrument these points using my new dispatcher.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --target 'onboarding_flow' --apply 'Insert analytics.track() calls at identified friction points.'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization Dashboards
steps.description: Create a simple admin view that displays your North Star Metric, ensuring you see the impact of your code immediately after deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lightweight React component that fetches and displays the last 7 days of 'User Signup' and 'Core Action' events from a mock API.

steps.title: Close the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Configure your deployment pipeline to log analytics status, ensuring every build maintains your tracking integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that verifies if the critical user events are fired when the corresponding UI elements are clicked.

pro_tips: Track only 3-5 high-value events; tracking everything is a form of procrastination.
Place analytics code in a separate folder to maintain clean separation of concerns.
Use 'feature flags' to toggle analytics intensity without redeploying your entire application.
Set up a 'Daily Digest' email that sends your North Star Metric to your inbox every morning.
If the data isn't moving, stop coding new features and focus exclusively on conversion optimization.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, ship your MVP to 10 beta testers, monitor the dashboard for 48 hours, and iterate based solely on the data—not your gut feeling.
content.conversion_block: You are sitting on a goldmine of potential, but only execution turns it into a business. Stop overthinking and start instrumenting your path to success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
